articles.title: Department of War Publishes Second Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
articles.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement. The Department says WAR.GOV/UFO has received over 1 billion hits worldwide since its May 8, 2026 launch, which it frames as evidence of major public interest in the topic and in the Trump administration's transparency effort. The statement adds that the Department of War and agency partners are working on a third UAP file release, which will be announced in the near future.

en.title: Department of War Publishes Second Release of Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Files on WAR.GOV/UFO
en.summary: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement.

de.title: Das Kriegsministerium veröffentlicht die zweite Veröffentlichung von Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen auf WAR.GOV/UFO
de.summary: Das Kriegsministerium gibt bekannt, dass es die zweite Veröffentlichung freigegebener und historischer Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen als Teil des Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) veröffentlicht. In der Pressemitteilung heißt es, dass die Sammlung weiterhin auf WAR.GOV/UFO gespeichert ist und dass nach und nach weitere Dateien veröffentlicht werden. Als Quelle der Aussage wird der Assistent des Kriegsministers für öffentliche Angelegenheiten und Chefsprecher des Pentagons, Sean Parnell, genannt.

articles.title: UAP forum will let public hear from lawmakers, whistleblowers on disclosure 2026 | JUSTICE - IUSTITIA.BG 2025
articles.summary: The Disclosure Forum 2026, held Thursday in Washington, D.C., brought together nearly two dozen lawmakers, former intelligence officials, researchers, and whistleblowers to discuss UAP disclosure. Panels and roundtables, themed “Humanity at the Edge of Discovery,” covered security, defense, and policy implications and featured speakers such as Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand, Rep. Eric Burlison, and Rep. Tim Burchett. Disclosure Foundation board members Professor Avi Loeb and Mike Gold spoke, with Gold noting that a poll shows 89 % of Republicans and 88 % of Democrats want more UAP information and describing UAP as a national security issue. Loeb, recently appointed by the White House to lead the UAP Science Advisory Council, said the federal government must allocate resources to analyze existing data and gather clearer new data on UAP. The forum also highlighted a 2015 Department of Defense video labeled Gimbal that shows an unexplained object, underscoring the need for further investigation. Some advisory council members are skeptical of UAP claims, indicating that belief in UAP is not universal among participants.

articles.title: As UFOs go mainstream, the jury is out on what the existence of alien life might mean for religion - Associated Press
articles.summary: As UFOs go mainstream, the jury is out on what the existence of alien life might mean for religion Posted Jun 12, 2026 08:01:58 AM. LOS ANGELES (AP) — In “Disclosure Day,” out Friday, Steven Spielberg is once again inviting audiences to ponder the existence of extraterrestrial life — and the implications it would have for religion on Earth. But Spielberg is hardly the only one making headlines of late about UFOs and the possibility of life on other planets. What was once considered fringe or conspiratorial has in recent months popped up everywhere from the White House to the Catholic Church, as public fascination with unidentified anomalous phenomena — or UAPs, as the government calls them — becomes more mainstream.

de.title: Da UFOs immer beliebter werden, ist sich die Jury nicht einig, was die Existenz außerirdischen Lebens für die Religion bedeuten könnte – Associated Press
de.summary: Da UFOs immer beliebter werden, ist sich die Jury nicht einig, was die Existenz außerirdischen Lebens für die Religion bedeuten könnte. Gepostet am 12. Juni 2026 um 08:01:58 Uhr. LOS ANGELES (AP) – In „Disclosure Day“, der am Freitag erscheint, lädt Steven Spielberg das Publikum erneut dazu ein, über die Existenz außerirdischen Lebens nachzudenken – und über die Auswirkungen, die es auf die Religion auf der Erde haben würde. Aber Spielberg ist nicht der Einzige, der in letzter Zeit Schlagzeilen über UFOs und die Möglichkeit von Leben auf anderen Planeten macht.

articles.title: New science advisory council forms to help US government ‘resolve the UAP mystery’
articles.summary: Investigative journalist Ross Coulthart said a skeptic on the new UAP Science Advisory Council could help the panel, but he worries that Michael Shermer, founder of The Skeptics Society, may be too biased against the topic. Coulthart cited Shermer’s pejorative remarks about whistleblower David Grusch, who claims the government has a secret program to retrieve alien technology, and said he prefers skeptics who are not debunkers. Shermer, who was named to the council, replied that he generally tries to be non‑disparaging and that Grusch deflects when asked for evidence, saying details are classified. The council, which also includes Harvard physicist Avi Loeb, will advise the federal government on how to collect better data on unidentified anomalous phenomena and assess potential national‑security threats. Shermer said most UFO sightings are explainable, wants detailed data to analyze, and believes it is unlikely that extraterrestrials have visited Earth, while offering to join Coulthart on a podcast for a thoughtful conversation.

articles.title: Disclosure Day (2026) Box Office performance crashes like a UFO in Roswell
articles.summary: Steven Spielberg's alien invasion film "Disclosure Day" opened in theaters on June 12, 2026. After just under two weeks, the movie has earned $162.7 million worldwide, with only $80.8 million coming from North America. The film's revenue fell 62 % in its second weekend, and its $115 million production cost means it would need about $280 million at the box office to break even. Because of the weak performance, Universal may pull the film from theatrical rotation if the trend continues, while the studio plans to accelerate online and digital releases. The movie stars Emily Blunt and is still currently playing in theaters, encouraging viewers to book tickets before it potentially closes.

articles.title: Newly released UFO files allege China and Russia retrieved downed UAPs and attempted to reverse-engineer them
articles.summary: Newly released UFO files made public on June 12 allege that China and Russia have retrieved downed UAPs and attempted to reverse‑engineer them. Jordan Flowers, executive director of the UAP Disclosure Foundation, said the files show foreign adversaries are engaged in research that could threaten U.S. national security. Flowers cited whistleblower David Grusch, who warned that the U.S. is aware of Russian and Chinese surveillance of its UAP programs and that a global race to reverse‑engineer the objects has extreme national‑security implications. The UAP Disclosure Foundation announced a forum in Washington, D.C., on June 25 to discuss the national‑security, technological, and religious implications of UAP disclosure. A CIA cable from July 2, 2008 placed U.S. assets in Zimbabwe on high alert after a UFO hovered over Harare airport, with beams and rotating lights, sparking debate over whether the craft was a foreign reconnaissance device or an extraterrestrial object.

de.title: In neu veröffentlichten UFO-Dateien wird behauptet, dass China und Russland abgeschossene UAPs zurückgeholt und versucht hätten, sie zurückzuentwickeln
de.summary: Neu veröffentlichte UFO-Dateien, die am 12. Juni veröffentlicht wurden, behaupten, dass China und Russland abgeschossene UAPs geborgen und versucht haben, sie zurückzuentwickeln. Jordan Flowers, Geschäftsführer der UAP Disclosure Foundation, sagte, die Akten zeigten, dass ausländische Gegner an Forschungsarbeiten beteiligt seien, die die nationale Sicherheit der USA gefährden könnten. Flowers zitierte den Whistleblower David Grusch, der warnte, dass die USA sich der Überwachung ihrer UAP-Programme durch Russland und China bewusst seien und dass ein weltweiter Wettlauf um die Rückentwicklung der Objekte extreme Auswirkungen auf die nationale Sicherheit habe.

summaries.file-release-department-of-war-publishes: The Department of War says it is publishing the second release of declassified and historical Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena files as part of the Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters, or PURSUE. The release states that the collection remains housed on WAR.GOV/UFO and that additional files will be released on a rolling basis. Assistant to the Secretary of War for Public Affairs and Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell is named as the source of the statement. The Department says WAR.GOV/UFO has received over 1 billion hits worldwide since its May 8, 2026 launch, which it frames as evidence of major public interest in the topic and in the Trump administration's transparency effort. The statement adds that the Department of War and agency partners are working on a third UAP file release, which will be announced in the near future.
summaries.program-new-science-advisory-council-for: Investigative journalist Ross Coulthart said a skeptic on the new UAP Science Advisory Council could help the panel, but he worries that Michael Shermer, founder of The Skeptics Society, may be too biased against the topic. Coulthart cited Shermer’s pejorative remarks about whistleblower David Grusch, who claims the government has a secret program to retrieve alien technology, and said he prefers skeptics who are not debunkers. Shermer, who was named to the council, replied that he generally tries to be non‑disparaging and that Grusch deflects when asked for evidence, saying details are classified. The council, which also includes Harvard physicist Avi Loeb, will advise the federal government on how to collect better data on unidentified anomalous phenomena and assess potential national‑security threats. Shermer said most UFO sightings are explainable, wants detailed data to analyze, and believes it is unlikely that extraterrestrials have visited Earth, while offering to join Coulthart on a podcast for a thoughtful conversation.
summaries.sighting-uap-forum-will-let-public-hear-: The Disclosure Forum 2026, held Thursday in Washington, D.C., brought together nearly two dozen lawmakers, former intelligence officials, researchers, and whistleblowers to discuss UAP disclosure. Panels and roundtables, themed “Humanity at the Edge of Discovery,” covered security, defense, and policy implications and featured speakers such as Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand, Rep. Eric Burlison, and Rep. Tim Burchett. Disclosure Foundation board members Professor Avi Loeb and Mike Gold spoke, with Gold noting that a poll shows 89 % of Republicans and 88 % of Democrats want more UAP information and describing UAP as a national security issue. Loeb, recently appointed by the White House to lead the UAP Science Advisory Council, said the federal government must allocate resources to analyze existing data and gather clearer new data on UAP. The forum also highlighted a 2015 Department of Defense video labeled Gimbal that shows an unexplained object, underscoring the need for further investigation. Some advisory council members are skeptical of UAP claims, indicating that belief in UAP is not universal among participants.
summaries.as-ufos-go-mainstream-the-jury-is-out-on: As UFOs go mainstream, the jury is out on what the existence of alien life might mean for religion Posted Jun 12, 2026 08:01:58 AM. LOS ANGELES (AP) — In “Disclosure Day,” out Friday, Steven Spielberg is once again inviting audiences to ponder the existence of extraterrestrial life — and the implications it would have for religion on Earth. But Spielberg is hardly the only one making headlines of late about UFOs and the possibility of life on other planets. What was once considered fringe or conspiratorial has in recent months popped up everywhere from the White House to the Catholic Church, as public fascination with unidentified anomalous phenomena — or UAPs, as the government calls them — becomes more mainstream.
summaries.program-ufo-conference-lands-in-canada-a: A leading scientific UFO conference will take place in Canada this summer. The Trump administration is continuing its rolling release of decades of unusual videos, images, and reports. The conference coincides with the release of these documents.
summaries.film-disclosure-day-2026-box-office-perf: Steven Spielberg's alien invasion film "Disclosure Day" opened in theaters on June 12, 2026. After just under two weeks, the movie has earned $162.7 million worldwide, with only $80.8 million coming from North America. The film's revenue fell 62 % in its second weekend, and its $115 million production cost means it would need about $280 million at the box office to break even. Because of the weak performance, Universal may pull the film from theatrical rotation if the trend continues, while the studio plans to accelerate online and digital releases. The movie stars Emily Blunt and is still currently playing in theaters, encouraging viewers to book tickets before it potentially closes.
summaries.file-release-newly-released-ufo-files-al: Newly released UFO files made public on June 12 allege that China and Russia have retrieved downed UAPs and attempted to reverse‑engineer them. Jordan Flowers, executive director of the UAP Disclosure Foundation, said the files show foreign adversaries are engaged in research that could threaten U.S. national security. Flowers cited whistleblower David Grusch, who warned that the U.S. is aware of Russian and Chinese surveillance of its UAP programs and that a global race to reverse‑engineer the objects has extreme national‑security implications. The UAP Disclosure Foundation announced a forum in Washington, D.C., on June 25 to discuss the national‑security, technological, and religious implications of UAP disclosure. A CIA cable from July 2, 2008 placed U.S. assets in Zimbabwe on high alert after a UFO hovered over Harare airport, with beams and rotating lights, sparking debate over whether the craft was a foreign reconnaissance device or an extraterrestrial object.

de.title: Das Kriegsministerium veröffentlicht die zweite Veröffentlichung von Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen auf WAR.GOV/UFO
de.summary: Das Kriegsministerium gibt bekannt, dass es die zweite Veröffentlichung freigegebener und historischer Dateien zu nicht identifizierten anomalen Phänomenen als Teil des Presidential Unsealing and Reporting System for UAP Encounters (PURSUE) veröffentlicht. In der Pressemitteilung heißt es, dass die Sammlung weiterhin auf WAR.GOV/UFO gespeichert ist und dass nach und nach weitere Dateien veröffentlicht werden. Als Quelle der Aussage wird der Assistent des Kriegsministers für öffentliche Angelegenheiten und Chefsprecher des Pentagons, Sean Parnell, genannt.

de.title: Im UAP-Forum wird die Öffentlichkeit von Gesetzgebern und Whistleblowern zur Offenlegung 2026 hören | GERECHTIGKEIT - IUSTITIA.BG 2025
de.summary: Das Disclosure Forum 2026, das am Donnerstag in Washington, D.C. stattfand, brachte fast zwei Dutzend Gesetzgeber, ehemalige Geheimdienstmitarbeiter, Forscher und Whistleblower zusammen, um über die Offenlegung von UAP zu diskutieren. In den Podiumsdiskussionen und Rundtischgesprächen unter dem Motto „Die Menschheit am Rande der Entdeckung“ ging es um Fragen zu Sicherheit, Verteidigung und Politik. Redner waren unter anderem Senatorin Kirsten Gillibrand, Rep.

de.title: Da UFOs immer beliebter werden, ist sich die Jury nicht einig, was die Existenz außerirdischen Lebens für die Religion bedeuten könnte – Associated Press
de.summary: Da UFOs immer beliebter werden, ist sich die Jury nicht einig, was die Existenz außerirdischen Lebens für die Religion bedeuten könnte. Gepostet am 12. Juni 2026 um 08:01:58 Uhr. LOS ANGELES (AP) – In „Disclosure Day“, der am Freitag erscheint, lädt Steven Spielberg das Publikum erneut dazu ein, über die Existenz außerirdischen Lebens nachzudenken – und über die Auswirkungen, die es auf die Religion auf der Erde haben würde. Aber Spielberg ist nicht der Einzige, der in letzter Zeit Schlagzeilen über UFOs und die Möglichkeit von Leben auf anderen Planeten macht.

Coulthart cited Shermer’s pejorative remarks about whistleblower David Grusch, who claims the government has a secret program to retrieve alien technology, and said he prefers skeptics who are not debunkers.

de.title: Neue wissenschaftliche Beiräte bilden sich, um der US-Regierung dabei zu helfen, „das UAP-Rätsel zu lösen“
de.summary: Der investigative Journalist Ross Coulthart sagte, ein Skeptiker des neuen UAP Science Advisory Council könnte dem Gremium helfen, er befürchtet jedoch, dass Michael Shermer, Gründer der Skeptics Society, zu voreingenommen gegenüber dem Thema sein könnte. Coulthart zitierte Shermers abwertende Bemerkungen über den Whistleblower David Grusch, der behauptet, die Regierung habe ein geheimes Programm zur Rückgewinnung außerirdischer Technologie, und sagte, er bevorzuge Skeptiker, die keine Entlarver seien.

de.title: Disclosure Day (2026) Die Vorstellung an den Kinokassen stürzt wie ein UFO in Roswell ab

Nach knapp zwei Wochen hat der Film weltweit 162,7 Millionen US-Dollar eingespielt, davon kamen nur 80,8 Millionen US-Dollar aus Nordamerika. Die Einnahmen des Films gingen am zweiten Wochenende um 62 % zurück, und die Produktionskosten von 115 Millionen US-Dollar bedeuten, dass an den Kinokassen etwa 280 Millionen US-Dollar nötig wären, um die Gewinnschwelle zu erreichen.
